text:
MAGNETIC RESONANCE IMAGING — BRAIN WITH AND WITHOUT GADOLINIUM CONTRAST

CLINICAL INDICATION:
Margaret Osei-Bonsu is a 58-year-old female with newly diagnosed Stage IV cutaneous malignant melanoma arising from the back (BRAF V600E-mutant, T4b N3c M1c, elevated LDH). Presenting features include bulky bilateral axillary lymphadenopathy (dominant left axillary mass measuring 4.8 cm on CT), multiple hepatic metastases, and subcutaneous satellite nodules along the left flank. Referred for brain MRI to complete staging evaluation and exclude central nervous system metastatic disease prior to initiation of systemic therapy. Concurrent CT chest/abdomen/pelvis demonstrates widespread visceral and nodal disease.

COMPARISON:
No prior brain imaging available for comparison.

TECHNIQUE:
Multiplanar, multisequence MRI of the brain was performed on a 3 Tesla magnet. Sequences acquired include: axial and sagittal T1-weighted, axial T2-weighted, axial FLAIR (fluid-attenuated inversion recovery), axial and coronal T2* gradient echo (susceptibility-weighted), axial diffusion-weighted imaging (DWI) with apparent diffusion coefficient (ADC) mapping, and axial, coronal, and sagittal T1-weighted post-contrast sequences following intravenous administration of 0.1 mmol/kg gadobutrol (Gadavist). No adverse reaction to contrast. Adequate image quality throughout. No motion degradation.

FINDINGS:

Supratentorial Brain:
The cerebral hemispheres are symmetric in size and configuration. The gray-white matter differentiation is preserved throughout both hemispheres. There is no focal cortical or subcortical signal abnormality to suggest parenchymal metastatic involvement. No areas of restricted diffusion are identified to suggest acute ischemia, cytotoxic edema, or cellular metastatic foci. No T2/FLAIR signal hyperintensity in the white matter beyond what would be expected for age. The corpus callosum is intact.

Post-Contrast Enhancement:
Following administration of gadolinium contrast, there is no abnormal parenchymal enhancement identified. Specifically, no ring-enhancing, nodular, or gyral enhancement is seen to suggest metastatic deposits. No leptomeningeal or sulcal enhancement is identified. No pachymeningeal (dural) enhancement to suggest dural-based metastasis.

Infratentorial Brain / Posterior Fossa:
The cerebellum is normal in signal and morphology bilaterally. The cerebellar hemispheres and vermis demonstrate no focal lesions, edema, or abnormal enhancement. The brainstem (midbrain, pons, and medulla) is unremarkable in size and signal. The fourth ventricle is normal in position and caliber.

Ventricular System and CSF Spaces:
The lateral ventricles, third ventricle, and fourth ventricle are symmetric and normal in size and morphology. There is no hydrocephalus, transependymal flow, or ventricular obstruction. The basal cisterns and subarachnoid spaces are open and patent. No evidence of CSF pathway obstruction.

Extra-Axial Spaces:
No subdural or epidural collections. No extra-axial masses. The calvarium and visualized skull base demonstrate normal signal characteristics without evidence of osseous metastatic involvement. No abnormal signal within the calvarium on T1 or T2 sequences to suggest marrow replacement by tumor.

Vascular Structures:
The major intracranial arterial flow voids appear maintained on the non-contrast sequences. No significant aneurysm or arteriovenous malformation is identified. No evidence of dural sinus thrombosis.

Incidental Findings:
There is a small well-defined T1 isointense and T2 hyperintense rounded structure in the pineal region measuring approximately 4 mm, consistent with a small benign pineal cyst. This is a common incidental finding with no clinical significance. No post-contrast enhancement associated with this cyst. No obstructive hydrocephalus.

IMPRESSION:

1. No evidence of intracranial metastatic disease. Specifically, no parenchymal brain metastases, no leptomeningeal carcinomatosis, and no pachymeningeal metastases are identified on this gadolinium-enhanced MRI.

2. No acute intracranial pathology. No hemorrhage, infarction, or significant mass effect.

3. Incidental small pineal cyst (approximately 4 mm), a benign and common incidental finding. No associated hydrocephalus or enhancement.

4. Otherwise unremarkable MRI of the brain.

These findings are communicated to the referring oncology team as part of the staging workup. The absence of detectable central nervous system involvement is noted in the context of M1c disease defined by elevated LDH and hepatic metastases.

Radiologist: [Electronically signed]
Attending Neuroradiologist
Department of Radiology
